why: why is javascript called a browser language
why run javascript in a browser
why it is called browser language
why js is called browser language
output: how to print hello world in js

1_beginner_explanation.summary: JavaScript (JS) is a high-level, dynamic, and interpreted programming language. Created to make static web pages interactive, it is the native programming language of the web browser. Over the years, JS has evolved to run on almost any platform, including servers, mobiles, and embedded systems.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: If HTML is the structural skeleton of a house (walls, doors) and CSS is the decoration (paint, wallpapers), then JavaScript is the active electrical and plumbing system (light switches, water taps). It adds behavior, interactivity, and life to the structure.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: Every web browser has a built-in engine (e.g. Google's V8, Mozilla's SpiderMonkey) to run JS. The engine parses the code, translates it into bytecode/machine code, and runs it on the CPU. The browser also provides Web APIs (like DOM, Fetch) to interact with the webpage.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: In browser environments, the JS runtime is single-threaded and runs along the UI thread. In headless runtimes like Node.js, it uses the V8 engine bound to the libuv C++ library to run outside the browser, enabling server-side execution and file system access.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Creating a button click handler that dynamically changes web page text in a browser.

4_common_confusion.trap: Thinking JavaScript is the same as Java, or that it is strictly a browser-only language.
4_common_confusion.fix: JavaScript and Java are entirely distinct languages. Also, JS is no longer limited to browsers; runtimes like Node.js and Deno let you build servers, APIs, and command-line tools with JS.

6_edge_case.quirk: Since JavaScript runs inside the browser environment, it does not have direct access to your computer's local files or system hardware for security reasons. Outside the browser (Node.js), this sandbox is removed, allowing full system access.
